{{Quote|That's Captain Ric Olié in ''Bravo One''. Battle-hardened at Chommell Minor.|Ars Veruna, pointing out ''Bravo 1'' to [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]]|<ref name="DP" />}}
[[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ric Olié/Legends|Ric Olié]] [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] the [[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]] '''''Bravo 1''''' as the [[Bravo Leader/Legends|leader]] of [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo Flight]], a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] in the [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps]]. ''Bravo 1'' was manufactured around the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]], and Olié later outfitted the [[Starship/Legends|craft]] with a personalized control cluster. In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], ''Bravo 1'' and the rest of Bravo Flight were interned in a [[Theed Hangar/Legends|hangar]] during the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|subjugation]] of Olié's [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]], the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. Once [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|freed]], Olié, in ''Bravo 1'', led his pilots in an [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|attempt]] to destroy [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitters]] on an [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Droid Control Ship]], the [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Palaa'']], that controlled the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Droid Army]] employed by the invading megacorporation. When [[Bravo Fighter|one]] of Bravo Squadron's fighters destroyed the entire Control Ship, the [[Droid/Legends|Droid]] Army was deactivated and Naboo was freed.

==Description==
{{Quote|The N-1 Starfighter represents the pinnacle in Naboo military technology.|Captain [[Quarsh Panaka/Legends|Quarsh Panaka]]|<ref name="Battle for NabooVG">''[[Star Wars: Episode I: Battle for Naboo]]''</ref>}}
''Bravo 1'' was an[[N-1 starfighter/Legends|N-1 starfighter]]<ref name="Card" /> designed by the [[Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps/Legends|Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps]]<ref name="CCS" /> and [[Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated/Legends|Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated]]<ref name="DP" /> for use in the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo's]] [[Naboo Royal Space Fighter Corps/Legends|Royal Space Fighter Corp]],<ref name="InsiderGuide" /> the [[Starfighter combat/Legends|dogfighting]] branch of the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]].<ref name="Ellberger" /> As it was flown in [[Monarch of Naboo/Legends|royal]] service, the [[Color/Legends|yellow]] [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]] was given a traditional [[Chromium/Legends|chromium]] finish across the bow and on its underside. The three-pronged [[Starship/Legends|starship]] was eleven [[Meter/Legends|meters]] long.<ref name="CCS" /> [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Ric Olié/Legends|Ric Olié]], who [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] ''Bravo 1'' as the [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of a [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] called [[Bravo Squadron/Legends|Bravo Flight]],<ref name="TPM" /> outfitted the vessel with a customized control cluster.<ref name="Card" />

Along the central prong rested a [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] that had room for a single pilot, immediately anterior to a space for an [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] and a pylon for connecting to [[Hangar/Legends|hangar]] [[Fuel/Legends|fueling]] stations.<ref name="CCS" /> There was [[Futhark/Legends|Futhark]] script on the outside of the cockpit to the pilot's left.<ref name="TWW">{{CSWECite|I||Futhark}} Here, Futhark script is identified. This was the same oval-shaped script that can be seen on the side of Olié's ship.</ref> Weapon systems were housed in front of the cockpit and consisted of a complement of eight [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]] and two [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] that fired green [[Laser/Legends|bolts]]. The torpedoes, released from two internal dispensors, fired from a single launch tube located under the craft's bow. The outer prongs were ''Bravo 1''{{'s}} wings, each sporting a [[221 sublight engine|221 sublight]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]]. For [[Hyperspace/Legends|faster-than-light travel]], it was propelled by a [[Monarc C-4 hyperdrive/Legends|Monarc C-4]] hyperdrive. ''Bravo 1'' was also equipped with [[Life support/Legends|life support]], [[Subspace transceiver/Legends|communication systems]],<ref name="CCS" /> [[Deflector shield/Legends|deflector shields]],<ref name="TPM" /> and a sophisticated [[Autopilot/Legends|autopilot]].<ref name="NEGVV" />

==History==
{{Quote|This is Bravo Leader. Bravo Two, intercept enemy fighters. Bravo Three, make your run on the transmitter station.|Ric Olié, commanding Bravo Squadron from ''Bravo 1''|<ref name="novel">[[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'' novelization]]</ref>}}
From his coronation, Naboo's [[King/Legends|King]] [[Ars Veruna/Legends|Ars Veruna]] worked with Nubia Star Drives, Incorporated and the Theed Palace Space Vessel Engineering Corps to design the N-1 starfighter. By the [[Time/Legends|time]] of his abdication of the throne<ref name="DP" /> in [[33 BBY/Legends|33 BBY]],<ref name="Veruna">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places Veruna's abdication of the throne in this year.</ref> Veruna had had some of the starfighters constructed. One of the first manufactured, ''Bravo 1'' was assigned to a new squadron, Bravo Flight, the first composed entirely of N-1s. ''Bravo 1'' was piloted by the squadron's leader, Ric Olié,<ref name="DP" /> under the [[Callsign/Legends|call sign]] [[Bravo Leader/Legends|Bravo Leader]],<ref name="NEGTC">''[[The New Essential Guide to Characters]]''</ref> and it saw [[Battle of Chommell Minor|combat]] at the planet [[Chommell Minor/Legends|Chommell Minor]] against a [[Unidentified pirate group (Chommell Minor)|group]] of [[Pirate/Legends|pirates]]. To test his new ship and the rest of Bravo Squadron, Olié led them on strafing runs destroying [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroids]].<ref name="DP" /> At some point, Olié personally modified the craft's control configurations to his liking.<ref name="Card" />

[[File:Bravo1.jpg|thumb|left|300px|''Bravo 1'' flying with Bravo Squadron]]
In [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' places the events of ''The Phantom Menace'' in this year.</ref> the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] responded to [[Senate Resolution BR-0371|increased taxation]] by the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] with an [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion and blockade]] of Naboo. ''Bravo 1'', alongside other Bravo starfighters and their pilots, was within [[Theed Hangar/Legends|Theed Hangar]] in the planetary [[Capital/Legends|capital]], [[Theed/Legends|Theed]], when the Federation took control of that building.<ref name="TPM" /> After a [[Standard month/Legends|month]] of failed negotiations, Veruna's successor as the Monarch of Naboo,<ref name="NEC text">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref> [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Padmé Amidala/Legends|Padmé Amidala]], was liberated from Federation custody by two [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]] and [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]. The trio and Amidala's security detail freed Theed Hangar so they could use her [[J-type 327 Nubian royal starship/Legends|personal craft]] to reach the [[The galaxy/Legends|galactic]] [[Capital/Legends|capital]], the planet of [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]], where she could petition the Galactic Republic's [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] for aid. Olié and the other pilots fled the hangar in their Queen's vessel and the Federation retook control of the building, along with ''Bravo 1'' and the other starfighters, in short order.<ref name="TPM" />

When her efforts in the Senate were stalled by bureaucratic [[Section 523A|practices]], the Queen decided to free her [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] with force; she led a task force to [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|storm]] her [[Theed Royal Palace/Legends|palace]]. One of her first steps was to free the hangar again so Bravo Squadron could claim its ships and disable the [[Vuutun Palaa/Legends|''Vuutun Palaa'']], an [[Orbit/Legends|orbiting]] [[Lucrehulk-class battleship/Legends|''Lucrehulk''-class Droid Control Ship]] that was commanding the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] forces stationed on Naboo.<ref name="TPM" /> Olié boarded ''Bravo 1'' with his personal [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]], [[R2-A6/Legends|R2-A6]],<ref name="FF" /> and his Squadron took flight.<ref name="TPM" /> While approaching the Control Ship, Olié [[Transmitter/Legends|transmitted]] the coordinates of the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|space battle]] to every N-1 starfighter in the area so any available ships could assist them.<ref name="Pilot">{{CCG|set=Theed Palace Limited|cardname=It's on Automatic Pilot!}}</ref> ''Bravo 1'' and Bravo Squadron skirmished with enemy [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|Vulture droids]]<ref name="TPM" /> and, upon an order from Olié, split into two forces—[[Bravo Flight A/Legends|one]] to continue to stave off the droids and [[Bravo Flight B/Legends|another]] to attack the Control Ship's transmitters.<ref name="Manga">[[Star Wars Manga: The Phantom Menace 2|''Star Wars Manga: The Phantom Menace'' 2]]</ref> ''Bravo 1'' was part of a strafing run, but its laser cannons could not pierce the Control Ship's deflector shields.<ref name="TPM" />

Meanwhile, a Bravo Squadron N-1 from the hangar that was not flown into battle,<ref name="TPM" /> ''[[Bravo Fighter]]'',<ref name="Fighter">{{CCG|set=Theed Palace Limited|cardname=Bravo Fighter}}</ref> received the coordinates from ''Bravo 1'',<ref name="Pilot" /> and its autopilot propelled it to that location. After some time, Vulture droids shot down the fighter and it crash-landed inside the Control Ship.<ref name="TPM" /> When it was brought to Olié's attention, he ordered his pilots to stay out of the Control Ship and to buy ''Bravo Fighter'' time to escape.<ref name="Battle for NabooVG" /> ''Bravo Fighter''{{'s}} pilot, a boy named [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], fired proton torpedoes into the ship's main [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]], destroying it. The destruction of the Control Ship deactivated all of the Trade Federation droids on the surface, giving Amidala leverage enough to compel the Federation to sign a treaty and release the planet.<ref name="TPM" /> Olié continued flying with Bravo Squadron for the next few [[Standard year/Legends|years]] afterward.<ref name="NEGTC" />

==Commanders and crew==
Ric Olié flew ''Bravo 1'' while fighting with Bravo Squadron.<ref name="Card" /> He was [[Reproduction/Legends|born]] on Naboo, and at the time of the Trade Federation's invasion he was considered the best pilot on that world.<ref name="NEGTC" /> Olié believed his [[R2-series astromech droid/Legends|R2-series astromech droid]], R2-A6, was a good [[luck]] charm, so he made sure to fly with that droid when piloting ''Bravo 1''.<ref name="FF" />

==Behind the scenes==
''Bravo 1'' first appeared in the [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] and later in the film itself, both of which were released in [[1999]].<ref name="TPM" /><ref name="novel" /> A [[December]] [[2001]] expansion of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game]], ''[[Theed Palace Limited]]'', contained a card called "Bravo 1" that identified the ship as such.<ref name="Card" /> The ship's first appearance outside the card and other material expanding upon ''Episode I'' was in [[2012]]'s [[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']] novel by [[James Luceno]].<ref name="DP" />
